title: quercus treleaseana
intro: Quercus treleaseana, also known as Trellease oak, is a species of oak tree native to Mexico and parts of Central America, belonging to the family Fagaceae. The bark and leaves of this plant are used in traditional medicine, with reported medicinal properties including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Quercus treleaseana include potential antiviral and antibacterial effects, as well as possible uses in the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Quercus treleaseana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
